title: Kubo-Ando Means and Rigidity of Quantum Positivity Cones
abstract: We investigate the stability of quantum positivity cones under nonlinear operator means. Specifically, we examine how Kubo--Ando means interact with the separable, positive partial transpose (PPT), and Schmidt-number cones. By analyzing the curvature of operator monotone functions at the identity, we give a strict rigidity phenomenon: weighted arithmetic means are the only Kubo--Ando means that preserve the separable cone in all dimensions. We show that the strictly positive curvature of any non-arithmetic mean explicitly forces a violation of the PPT condition, even in the foundational two-qubit setting, and can strictly increase the Schmidt number of the resulting operator. Finally, using the Choi--Jamiołkowski correspondence, we translate these geometric obstructions to the map-theoretic setting, concluding that convex mixing is the uniquely permissible Kubo--Ando operation for preserving entanglement-breaking quantum channels.
summary: This paper demonstrates that only standard convex mixing (arithmetic means) preserves the essential entanglement-related properties of quantum states and channels. It shows that any other type of Kubo-Ando averaging can destroy separability or increase the complexity of entanglement (Schmidt number).
main_problem: The paper investigates the stability and rigidity of quantum positivity cones (separable, PPT, and Schmidt-number cones) under the action of nonlinear Kubo-Ando operator means.
main_result: The authors prove that weighted arithmetic means are the only Kubo-Ando means that preserve the separable cone and the class of entanglement-breaking channels; all other means can increase Schmidt numbers or violate the PPT condition.
method: The study uses curvature analysis of operator monotone functions, Taylor expansions, and the construction of explicit two-qubit counterexamples, extended to higher dimensions via canonical isometries and the Choi-Jamiołkowski correspondence.
model_or_system: The mathematical framework involves bipartite tensor product spaces (C^m tensor C^n) and the study of operator means acting on positive operators and quantum channels.
key_observables: Schmidt number, PPT (positive partial transpose) property, and separability.
important_parameters: The curvature of the operator monotone function (kappa_sigma), where kappa_sigma = 0 corresponds to arithmetic means and kappa_sigma > 0 corresponds to non-arithmetic means.
main_assumptions: The results specifically address the behavior of non-arithmetic Kubo-Ando means and assume the use of the Choi-Jamiołkowski correspondence to relate operators to maps.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the curvature of Kubo-Ando means, moves to proving rigidity theorems for separable and PPT cones using two-qubit counterexamples, extends these results to Schmidt-number cones, and concludes by translating these findings to the theory of quantum channels.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for researchers in open quantum systems and quantum information, as it establishes fundamental limits on how non-linear averaging processes can affect entanglement and the structural integrity of quantum channels.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
